title: GetResponse vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse vs Drip: pricing, features, and which email marketing platform suits your business needs.
h1: GetResponse vs Drip: Complete Comparison for 2025
intro: GetResponse and Drip are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. GetResponse is a versatile all-in-one platform ideal for course creators and webinar hosts, while Drip specializes in e-commerce email marketing with deep store integration. Your choice depends on your business model and the specific features you need.

comparison_table.tool_a: Course creators, webinar hosts, online businesses

comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, SMS, onsite campaigns

choose_a_if: You're an online course creator or webinar host needing an all-in-one platform
You want a free tier to test email marketing before paying
You need landing pages, funnels, and automation in one tool
You're budget-conscious and want affordable pricing starting at $19/month
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep platform integration (Shopify, WooCommerce, etc.)
You want revenue attribution and detailed e-commerce tracking
You need SMS marketing and onsite campaigns alongside email
You prioritize specialized e-commerce features over general-purpose tools

faq.a: It depends on your needs. GetResponse is better for course creators, webinar hosts, and small businesses due to its versatility and free tier. Drip is better for e-commerce businesses needing specialized features like revenue attribution and store integration. Both have strong ratings, with Drip slightly ahead on G2 (4.4 vs 4.2), but GetResponse offers more value for non-e-commerce use cases.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Drip?
faq.a: GetResponse is significantly cheaper, starting at $0 with its free tier (up to 500 subscribers) and paid plans beginning at $19/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. If you're budget-conscious or just starting out, GetResponse offers better entry-level pricing.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms support data export/import. You can export your subscriber list and automation workflows from Drip and import them into GetResponse. We recommend backing up your data and testing the migration process carefully to ensure no contacts or campaigns are lost during the transition.
